channel.title: Timelines on My blog
channel.description: Recent content in Timelines on My blog

item.title: 1st Patented Gasoline Car
item.description: An economist ahead of its time “Are we wise in allowing the commerce of this country to rise beyond the point at which we can long maintain it?” asked rhetorically the british economist Stanley Jevons in 1865 in his book The Coal Question: An Inquiry Concerning the Progress of the Nation, and the Probable Exhaustion of Our Coal Mines . Mr Jevons was a visionnary, indeed he was already conscious of three important points:

item.title: The Coal Question
item.description: An economist ahead of its time “Are we wise in allowing the commerce of this country to rise beyond the point at which we can long maintain it?” asked rhetorically the british economist Stanley Jevons in 1865 in his book The Coal Question: An Inquiry Concerning the Progress of the Nation, and the Probable Exhaustion of Our Coal Mines . Mr Jevons was a visionnary, indeed he was already conscious of three important points:
